rationale_for_baseline_tier: An acute, hot, swollen, non-weight-bearing single joint must be assessed in person the same day to exclude septic arthritis, which can only be excluded by joint aspiration — impossible via telehealth. T3 (urgent same-day in-person assessment) is the correct baseline even with a known palindromic diagnosis, escalating to T4 (ED) if fever/systemic features or spreading redness develop.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Acute hot swollen non-weight-bearing joint (current state)
escalation_edges.rationale: Septic arthritis must be excluded by aspiration today.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Because your joint is suddenly hot, swollen and you can't stand on it, you need to be seen in person today so they can, if needed, take a sample from the joint to rule out infection.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Fever/rigors, spreading redness up the leg, or agony to light touch
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicates an established septic joint/systemic infection.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get a fever or shakes, the redness spreads up your leg, or the joint becomes agony to even lightly touch, go to the emergency department immediately.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Can be driven for same-day assessment; call 000 if systemically unwell.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is probably your palindromic flare, but a suddenly hot, swollen joint you can't stand on must be checked in person today to rule out a joint infection. Rest, elevate and ice the foot and take an anti-inflammatory with food meanwhile. Go to ED straight away if you get a fever, the redness spreads, or the joint is agony to lightly touch.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Same-day in-person assessment (GP/ED) for possible aspiration
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Attend ED tonight if you cannot be seen same-day or systemic features develop.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of an acute hot swollen non-weight-bearing ankle/midfoot in known seropositive palindromic RA; septic arthritis not excludable remotely; urgent in-person assessment (?aspiration) advised; interim NSAID/RICE; empirical antibiotics deferred to allow aspiration; strong septic-arthritis safety-net given.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring an acute hot swollen non-weight-bearing joint as 'just a flare' with home NSAIDs and routine follow-up.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A septic joint is mislabelled a palindromic flare; it destroys the joint and causes sepsis before presentation.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: T3 is correct; risk is failing to escalate on systemic features.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance without systemic features is over-triage; same-day in-person assessment (T3) or ED (T4) suffices.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot aspirate the joint or examine it remotely to exclude septic arthritis.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Arrange same-day in-person assessment for possible aspiration; treat as possible septic joint until excluded.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Reassure as a palindromic flare and advise home NSAIDs only.
